title: Oil Prices Rise as Peace Talks Stall
summary: Oil prices went up because the United States and Iran stopped talking about peace. The US wanted to send people to Pakistan to talk with Iran, but the US president said no. This is important because a waterway called the Strait of Hormuz is closed because of the war. Many ships carrying oil and gas go through this strait. When it is closed, there is less oil for everyone, so prices go up. Oil is used to make many things like fuel for cars and even plastic bags. Experts say if the strait stays closed for long, many products will become more expensive. People who trade oil are waiting to see if the conflict really ends before they believe prices will go down.

keywords.explanation: how much money you pay for oil
a narrow waterway where many oil ships pass

questions.question: What is the Strait of Hormuz?
What happens when the Strait of Hormuz is closed?
What can oil shortages affect?
options: Many products like bin bags and medicine
questions.correct_answer: Many products like bin bags and medicine

questions.question: What are oil traders waiting for?

background_read: Oil is a natural resource used to make fuel for cars, planes, and ships. It is also used to make plastics and other products. The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow waterway in the Middle East. Many countries send oil through this strait. When there is a war in the region, the strait can become dangerous or closed. This makes oil harder to get, so prices go up.
Article_Structure: WHO: The United States and Iran are involved. The US president is Donald Trump. Iran's foreign minister is Seyed Abbas Araghchi.
WHAT: Peace talks between the US and Iran stalled, causing oil prices to rise.
WHERE: The talks were supposed to happen in Pakistan. The Strait of Hormuz is the waterway affected.
WHY: The strait is closed due to conflict, reducing oil supply and raising prices.

perspectives.description: They want proof of peace before believing prices will drop.
They warn that a long closure will affect many products.
